headline: 5 regulatory agencies share overlapping jurisdiction on fintech
context: Five federal agencies — Bank Negara, Securities Commission, MDEC, MCMC, and the Finance Ministry — claim overlapping regulatory authority over fintech companies, creating compliance chaos.

cards.big: 5 agencies regulate fintech — companies face contradictory requirements from each
cards.sub: Regulatory overlap creates compliance nightmare for Malaysia's growing fintech sector

cards.big: No single legislation defines fintech regulatory boundaries between agencies
cards.sub: Each agency claims jurisdiction under its own enabling act without coordination

cards.big: Malaysian fintech companies spend average RM1.2 million annually on multi-agency compliance
cards.sub: Regulatory cost burden is 3 times higher than equivalent Singapore companies

cards.big: Inter-agency coordination committee has met only twice since formation in 2023
cards.sub: Turf protection prevents agencies from cooperating on unified regulatory framework

cards.big: 5 regulators isn't 5 times the oversight — it's 5 times the confusion and zero the accountability
Should Malaysia create a single fintech regulatory sandbox under one lead agency?
